Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Vasyl, Andre.

This is a question for discussion.
Does the exception being serialized during ui-ci workflow? If they do serialization is required. If they do not, it is an overhead which is not useful and lead for bugs.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> Vasyl, Andre.
> 
> This is a question for discussion.
> Does the exception being serialized during ui-ci workflow? If they do serialization is required. If they do not, it is an overhead which is not useful and lead for bugs.

As far as I'm aware of, they are not being serialized.
I was really doing this because of sonar recommendations that I believe I saw in another environment: https://rules.sonarsource.com/java/RSPEC-2057. As I did in other exception classes, Vasyl pointed out to do in the remaining.
If it can lead to bugs, I have no problem in removing those ids. Just out of curiosity, could you please provide information on what bugs we could have?

> > 
> > Vasyl, Andre.
> > 
> > This is a question for discussion.
> > Does the exception being serialized during ui-ci workflow? If they do serialization is required. If they do not, it is an overhead which is not useful and lead for bugs.

> As far as I'm aware of, they are not being serialized.
> I was really doing this because of sonar recommendations that I believe I saw in another environment: https://rules.sonarsource.com/java/RSPEC-2057. As I did in other exception classes, Vasyl pointed out to do in the remaining.
> If it can lead to bugs, I have no problem in removing those ids. Just out of curiosity, could you please provide information on what bugs we could have?

I would recommend to remove it and add the suppress warning.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> > Patch Set 4:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 4:
> > > 
> > > (1 comment)
> > > 
> > > Vasyl, Andre.
> > > 
> > > This is a question for discussion.
> > > Does the exception being serialized during ui-ci workflow? If they do serialization is required. If they do not, it is an overhead which is not useful and lead for bugs.
> > 
> > As far as I'm aware of, they are not being serialized.
> > I was really doing this because of sonar recommendations that I believe I saw in another environment: https://rules.sonarsource.com/java/RSPEC-2057. As I did in other exception classes, Vasyl pointed out to do in the remaining.
> > If it can lead to bugs, I have no problem in removing those ids. Just out of curiosity, could you please provide information on what bugs we could have?
> 
> I would recommend to remove it and add the suppress warning.

What is the harm of leaving there if a Throwable is a Serializable class and is strongly recommended to add a serialVersionUID in a Serializable class?
Also they are all new classes.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> > Patch Set 4:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 4:
> > > 
> > > > Patch Set 4:
> > > > 
> > > > (1 comment)
> > > > 
> > > > Vasyl, Andre.
> > > > 
> > > > This is a question for discussion.
> > > > Does the exception being serialized during ui-ci workflow? If they do serialization is required. If they do not, it is an overhead which is not useful and lead for bugs.
> > > 
> > > As far as I'm aware of, they are not being serialized.
> > > I was really doing this because of sonar recommendations that I believe I saw in another environment: https://rules.sonarsource.com/java/RSPEC-2057. As I did in other exception classes, Vasyl pointed out to do in the remaining.
> > > If it can lead to bugs, I have no problem in removing those ids. Just out of curiosity, could you please provide information on what bugs we could have?
> > 
> > I would recommend to remove it and add the suppress warning.
> 
> What is the harm of leaving there if a Throwable is a Serializable class and is strongly recommended to add a serialVersionUID in a Serializable class?
> Also they are all new classes.

1. True - it is not that critical. And can be committed. 
2. What is wrong:
 It is a hint that serialization is used. So if someone add/delete/remove a field from this class he needs to create a new serialVersionId - because it's a new version.

> > What is the harm of leaving there if a Throwable is a Serializable class and is strongly recommended to add a serialVersionUID in a Serializable class?
> > Also they are all new classes.
> 
> 1. True - it is not that critical. And can be committed. 
> 2. What is wrong:
>  It is a hint that serialization is used. So if someone add/delete/remove a field from this class he needs to create a new serialVersionId - because it's a new version.

Ok, agreed, I see the point. Some changes requires to change the id: https://docs.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/platform/serialization/spec/version.html#6678
I will add the suppression, thanks.
